compressed_prompt:
I am a software engineer with 7 years of experience, and I am looking for a new job. Can you create a resume for me and explain each section?

create resume, no jargon, explain acronyms
response:
# Professional Resume Guide & Template

I'll create a **clean, professional resume** for you and explain each section clearly.

---

## ⚠️ Important Note First
I don't know your specific details, so I'll use **placeholder information** in brackets like **[Your Name]**. Replace these with your real information.

---

# THE RESUME

---

**[YOUR FULL NAME]**
[Your City, State] | [Your Phone Number] | [Your Email Address]
[LinkedIn Profile Link] | [GitHub Profile Link]

---

## PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
*A 2-3 sentence snapshot of who you are professionally*

> Software Engineer with 7 years of experience building and maintaining web applications. Skilled in writing clean code, solving complex technical problems, and working with cross-functional teams. Looking for a role where I can build reliable software and grow as a technical leader.

**Why this section matters:** Hiring managers spend about 6 seconds on a first glance. This section immediately tells them if you are worth reading further.

---

## TECHNICAL SKILLS
*A list of tools and technologies you know*

**Programming Languages** *(the coding languages you write in)*
- Python, JavaScript, Java *(replace with yours)*

**Frameworks** *(pre-built toolkits that speed up development)*
- React, Node.js, Django *(replace with yours)*

**Databases** *(software used to store and organize data)*
- PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB *(replace with yours)*

**Cloud Platforms** *(remote computer servers you deploy software to)*
- AWS *(Amazon Web Services)*, GCP *(Google Cloud Platform)* *(replace with yours)*

**Tools**
- Git *(version control - tracks changes in your code)*, Docker *(packages software so it runs the same everywhere)*, CI/CD *(Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment - automated testing and releasing of code)*

**Why this section matters:** Many companies use ATS *(Applicant Tracking Systems)* - software that scans resumes for keywords before a human ever reads it. Listing your skills clearly helps you pass this filter.

---

## WORK EXPERIENCE
*Your job history, starting with the most recent job first*

---

**Senior Software Engineer**
[Company Name] — [City, State or Remote]
[Month Year] – Present

- Led development of a customer-facing web application that reduced page load time by 40%
- Mentored 3 junior engineers *(less experienced team members)* through weekly code reviews *(checking each other's code for errors and improvements)*
- Designed and built REST APIs *(Application Programming Interfaces - systems that let different software talk to each other)* serving 500,000 daily users
- Reduced system downtime *(when the software stops working)* by 25% by improving monitoring and alert systems
- Collaborated with Product Managers and Designers to deliver features on schedule

---

**Software Engineer**
[Company Name] — [City, State or Remote]
[Month Year] – [Month Year]

- Built and maintained features for an e-commerce platform processing $2M in monthly transactions
- Wrote automated tests *(code that checks if other code works correctly)* that increased code coverage *(percentage of code being tested)* from 45% to 80%
- Migrated *(moved)* legacy database *(old, outdated system)* to a modern cloud-based solution, improving query speed *(how fast data is retrieved)* by 60%
- Participated in Agile sprints *(Agile is a work style where teams work in short 2-week cycles called sprints to deliver small pieces of work regularly)*

---

**Junior Software Engineer**
[Company Name] — [City, State or Remote]
[Month Year] – [Month Year]

- Developed internal tools that saved the operations team 10 hours of manual work per week
- Fixed bugs *(errors in code)* and improved performance of existing web applications
- Learned and applied best practices *(proven, recommended ways of doing things)* for writing maintainable code *(code that is easy to update and understand later)*

---

**Why this section matters:** This is the most important section. Use **numbers and results** whenever possible. "Improved speed by 60%" is far stronger than "improved speed." Show what you built AND the impact it had.

---

## EDUCATION
*Your academic background*

**Bachelor of Science in Computer Science** *(or your actual degree)*
[University Name] — [City, State]
Graduated: [Year]

**Why this section matters:** Many job postings list a degree as a requirement. Even if your experience matters more at 7 years, always include it.

---

## PROJECTS *(Optional but Recommended)*
*Personal or side projects you built outside of work*

**[Project Name]**
- Built a [brief description] using [technologies used]
- GitHub: [link to your code] | Live Demo: [link if available]

**Why this section matters:** Projects show passion and initiative. They also demonstrate skills you may not use at your current job.

---

## CERTIFICATIONS *(Optional)*
*Official credentials that prove a specific skill*

- AWS Certified Solutions Architect *(proves knowledge of Amazon cloud services)* — [Year]
- [Any other certifications you hold]

---

# KEY RESUME TIPS

| Tip | Why It Matters |
|-----|---------------|
| Keep it to **1-2 pages** | Hiring managers are busy |
| Use **bullet points**, not paragraphs | Easier to scan quickly |
| Use **action verbs** to start bullets (Built, Led, Designed, Reduced) | Sounds confident and clear |
| **Tailor it** for each job application | Match keywords from the job description |
| **No spelling errors** | Shows attention to detail |
| Save as a **PDF** *(Portable Document Format)* | Looks the same on every computer |

---

## WHAT TO DO NEXT

1. **Copy this template** into Google Docs or Microsoft Word
2. **Replace all brackets** with your real information
3. **Add your actual numbers** - think about traffic, users, money, time saved, percentage improvements
4. **Remove sections** that don't apply to you
5. **Proofread** - read it out loud to catch errors

---

Would you like me to help you with any specific section, tailor this for a specific job posting, or help you write stronger bullet points for your experience?
